attacking_style: Rarely initiates attacks; prefers to follow wheels and respond to moves on mountain stages, showing patience to finish from select groups rather than solo efforts.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on domestique positioning and tempo support; exposed when team cannot control positioning or protect through technical sections. Benefits from Ineos train when available but vulnerable without it.
terrain_preference: Pure climbing stages with steep gradients (2km+ climbs) where sustained power excels; struggles on rolling terrain requiring repeated accelerations and exposed on technical descents and flat positioning battles.
vulnerability: Poor positioning awareness in peloton—dropped positions twice due to crashes/splits (3.7km crash, 4th to 8th GC). Wet descents pose crash risk. Lacks decisive punch to follow explosive attacks.
key_patterns: Superior climbing splits but slower on flats—selective power allocation favoring sustained climbs (3 observations)
Positioning errors cost GC places: caught behind crashes, lost 4 positions on 'easy' stage (3 observations)
Follow-wheels tactical approach: waits for others to attack, then responds from group (4 observations)
Time-bonus hunting awareness when in GC contention shows tactical maturity (2 observations)
Crash exits from promising GC positions expose fragility in three-week races (2 Grand Tours)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Giro showed strong climbing form (fastest splits) but crash exit and earlier positioning losses suggest declining race craft and team support compared to 2020 win
